title: Buttery Gooey Spanish Bok Choi Paella
description: A quick-fix Spanish dish featuring bok choi, with buttery and gooey textures. This pressure-cooked recipe is perfect for a weeknight dinner.
instructions: Heat 2 tablespoons of butter in a large pressure cooker over medium-high heat.
Add the chopped bok choi stems and cook until tender, about 3 minutes.
Add the chopped bok choi leaves, smoked paprika, garlic powder, salt, and black pepper. Cook for 1 minute.
Add the Spanish rice, chicken broth, and remaining 2 tablespoons of butter. Stir to combine.
Close the pressure cooker and cook for 5 minutes.
Let the pressure release naturally for 5 minutes, then quick-release any remaining pressure.
Fluff the paella with a fork and serve immediately.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
